pi_items.source: Consistent with task description final line ('Save as /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/ant_experiment.psyexp') and setup_task.sh line 17 which deletes any pre-existing file at this path. export_result.sh line 18 reads from this exact path. Verified during live testing that /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/ directory exists with correct permissions (drwxr-xr-x ga:ga).

Task description specifies 'ant_conditions.csv'. setup_task.sh lines 17-19 delete both primary and fallback paths. export_result.sh lines 19-20 check both paths. Verified during live testing that both directories exist and are writable by user ga.

These columns directly correspond to the four factors of the Fan et al. (2002) ANT factorial design (cue type, flanker type, target location, target direction) plus the correct response key (corrAns). Verified against multiple independent sources including the Millisecond ANT Library, PMC fatigue study (PMC4159443), and the Frontiers ANT Database review (10.3389/fpsyg.2020.00388). The column names follow standard PsychoPy conventions for conditions files.

Arithmetic verification: 4 cue types x 3 flanker types x 2 target locations x 2 target directions = 48. This matches the published ANT design: the PMC fatigue study (PMC4159443) explicitly states '4 cue conditions x 2 target locations x 2 central arrow directions x 3 flanker conditions x 2 repetitions = 96 trials per block', confirming 48 unique conditions with 2 repetitions. The Millisecond ANT manual confirms the same factorial structure.

Fan et al. (2002) defined exactly four cue conditions: no cue (no warning), center cue (asterisk at fixation), double cue (asterisks at both possible target locations), and spatial cue (asterisk at actual target location, 100% valid). Confirmed by Millisecond ANT Library, PMC ANT-R paper (PMC2674119), NITRC Rockland Sample documentation, and Frontiers ANT Database review. The label 'none' for no-cue is the standard naming convention across published implementations.

Fan et al. (2002) defined three flanker conditions: congruent (all arrows same direction, e.g. >>>>>), incongruent (center arrow opposite to flankers, e.g. >><>>), and neutral (center arrow flanked by horizontal lines/dashes, e.g. --<--). Confirmed by Millisecond ANT Library, PMC fatigue study (PMC4159443: 'flanker arrows that can point in the same direction as the central arrow (congruent), in the opposite direction (incongruent), or in neither direction (straight lines; neutral condition)'), and TaskBeacon ANT registry.

Fan et al. (2002) presented targets at one of two locations: above or below the central fixation cross. Confirmed by Millisecond ANT manual ('Locations: Above or below fixation cross (2 positions)'), PMC fatigue study ('2 target locations (above or below fixation)'), and NITRC documentation. Target eccentricity was 1.06 degrees of visual angle above or below fixation in the original paper.

Fan et al. (2002) used left-pointing and right-pointing arrows as targets. Participants pressed the corresponding arrow key. Confirmed by Millisecond manual ('Directions: Left or right pointing arrows (2 directions)'), PMC ANT-R paper ('half were pointing to the left and half to the right'), and NITRC documentation ('indicate the direction (left or right) of a central arrow').

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task where the agent must build a complete Attentional Network Test (ANT) experiment from scratch in PsychoPy Builder. No pre-existing data is provided — the agent must create both the conditions CSV and the .psyexp experiment file. The ANT paradigm (Fan et al., 2002, J Cogn Neurosci 14(3):340-347) has a precise factorial design: 4 cue types (none, center, double, spatial) x 3 flanker types (congruent, incongruent, neutral) x 2 target locations (above, below fixation) x 2 target directions (left, right) = 48 unique conditions. The conditions CSV must have exactly these 5 columns: cue_type, flanker_type, target_location, target_direction, corrAns. The corrAns column must match target_direction (i.e., 'left' when target points left, 'right' when target points right). Trial timing from the original paper: variable fixation 400-1600ms (uniform random), cue flash 100ms, post-cue fixation 400ms, target+flankers displayed until response or 1700ms timeout. The experiment must include: instructions routine, practice block with per-trial accuracy feedback, rest screen, three main experimental blocks each looping over the conditions file with rest screens between blocks, and a summary routine showing mean RT and accuracy. A Code component is required for: (a) randomizing fixation duration each trial, (b) constructing flanker+target display from condition variables, (c) controlling cue visibility by cue_type, and (d) computing summary statistics. The experiment file must be saved as /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/ant_experiment.psyexp. The conditions CSV should be at /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/ant_conditions.csv. Response keys are left and right arrow keys. Congruent flankers: all arrows same direction (e.g., <<<<<). Incongruent: center arrow opposite (e.g., >><>>). Neutral: center arrow with dashes (e.g., --<--).
